description:
## Problem
`src/handlers/health.ts` uses `readdirSync` (synchronous, blocks the Node.js event loop) inside async functions `readLatestHistoryFile` and `getHealthTrend`. In a stdio MCP server, blocking the event loop stalls ALL pending requests.

Line 165 in health.ts: blocking call inside an async function:
```
const files = readdirSync(dir).filter(f => f.endsWith('.json')).sort().reverse();
```

Similarly, `existsSync` is used to check directory existence inside async functions.

## Fix
Replace all sync I/O in async functions with their async counterparts from `node:fs/promises`:
- `readdirSync(dir)` -> `await readdir(dir)`
- `existsSync(dir)` -> use `SafeFileOperations.fileExists()` which is already async

The `SafeFileOperations.fileExists()` in `shared/file-ops.ts` already provides an async exists check.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `src/handlers/health.ts` uses only async fs operations in async functions
- No `readdirSync` or `existsSync` in async code paths
- All 27 health handler tests still pass
title: Replace blocking readdirSync with async readdir in health.ts
